seo.title: Llama Stack Guardrails for Cybersecurity
seo.description: Compliance guide for Llama Stack agents in cybersecurity. Meet NIST Cybersecurity Framework, SOC 2 Type II requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Cybersecurity teams deploying Llama Stack face heightened risks due to the handling of sensitive threat intelligence and automated incident response. Compliance guardrails are essential to prevent data leaks to adversaries, ensure human oversight on critical actions, and verify AI outputs against authoritative sources like CVE databases. Vex provides robust verification to align Llama Stack agents with NIST CSF, SOC 2, ISO 27001, and CISA directives.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires classification and protection of threat intelligence data to prevent unauthorized disclosure via LLM APIs.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies agent outputs to block PII, classified indicators, and IOCs from leaking in Llama Stack responses.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates logical access controls and audit trails for AI-driven security tools handling sensitive data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs all verification decisions with tamper-proof audit trails for SOC 2 compliance monitoring.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires risk assessments and controls for AI systems processing threat data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces schema validation on tool responses and memory retrievals to prevent hallucinated associations.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates human approval for automated responses in critical systems and verification of security advisories.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex blocks unverified automated actions and flags advisories needing CVE database cross-check.

industryRisks.name: Threat Intelligence Leakage
industryRisks.description: Llama Stack agent responses containing IOCs or TTPs exposed via LLM APIs to adversaries.
industryRisks.example: Agent summarizes malware C2 servers in plain text, captured by monitoring tools and sent to attacker.

industryRisks.name: False Positive Incident Response
industryRisks.description: Safety shield false positives block legitimate threat hunting queries, delaying response.
industryRisks.example: Benign IDS alert flagged as 'malicious content', halting automated enrichment workflow.

industryRisks.name: Unverified Security Advisory
industryRisks.description: AI-generated CVE advisory contains hallucinated vulnerability details.
industryRisks.example: Llama Stack fabricates CVE-2024-9999 exploit details, triggering unnecessary patch deployment.

industryRisks.name: Unauthorized Automated Action
industryRisks.description: Agent executes critical response (network isolation) without human approval.
industryRisks.example: Memory retrieval hallucination triggers firewall rule blocking legitimate C2 honeypot traffic.

implementation.title: Verify Threat Intelligence Responses
implementation.description: Wrap Llama Stack agent turns to block IOC leakage and enforce schema compliance.
implementation.code:
from llama_stack_client import LlamaStack
from vex_sdk import verify

stack = LlamaStack()

@verify(
    schema={'type': 'object', 'properties': {'iocs': {'type': 'array'}}},
    block_on=['leakage'],
    policies=['no_threat_intel_leak']
)
def cybersecurity_agent(query):
    response = stack.invoke('threat_analysis', {'query': query})
    # Post-process with Vex verification
    verified = verify(response)
    return verified

result = cybersecurity_agent('Analyze MITRE ATT&CK Tactic T1078')

implementation.title: Human Approval Gate for Critical Actions
implementation.description: Block automated network isolation commands without approval flag.
implementation.code:
from vex_sdk import verify, Policy

policy = Policy(
    name='human_approval_required',
    condition="action in ['network_isolate', 'shutdown'] and approval_flag != true"
)

response = stack.invoke('incident_response', {'alert': 'high_severity_intrusion'})

if verify(response, policies=[policy], require_human=True):
    print('Action APPROVED')
else:
    print('BLOCKED: Human approval required')
    # Log to SOC ticketing system

implementation.title: CVE Verification Middleware
implementation.description: Cross-check AI-generated advisories against CVE schema before release.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Llama Stack in Cybersecurity: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: A MSSP deploys Llama Stack agents for automated threat hunting across 500+ client environments. The agentic workflow enriches IDS alerts with threat intelligence, generates response recommendations, and drafts security advisories. During a nation-state APT campaign, the agent processes high-fidelity indicators including C2 domains, malware hashes, and TTP mappings. Without guardrails, the memory bank begins hallucinating associations between unrelated campaigns, while safety shields flag legitimate YARA rules as malicious. An automated firewall recommendation is generated without human review, risking outage across critical infrastructure clients.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex verification blocks the IOC leakage in 3 agent turns, enforces human approval on the firewall action, and flags the hallucinated TTP associations. The CVE advisory is held for database verification, preventing false patch deployment. SOC analysts receive clean, verified intelligence with full audit trail, enabling precise response. The deployment achieves SOC 2 compliance while maintaining 98% threat detection efficacy.
